text.title: reranking-pipeline-integration Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of reranking-pipeline-integration enforces.

rule.statement: Expose a single RerankingRAG class with .retrieve(query, top_k) → list[{id, score, source}]; never leak backend-specific shapes upstream.
rule.rationale: Backend-specific shapes leak into callers and lock the design in.

rule.statement: Wrap reranker call in a circuit breaker (default 5 failures in 60s opens; 30s cooldown).
rule.rationale: Cascading failures from a flaky reranker take down the whole pipeline.

rule.statement: RerankingRAG.__init__ calls warmup once (per r3 in reranking-models).
Log retrieval_ms and rerank_ms separately; aggregate to identify bottleneck.
rule.rationale: Merged latency obscures the actual culprit.

rule.statement: On reranker failure, return ANN top-K with a result.degraded=true flag; never raise.
rule.rationale: Downstream needs to know it's reading a degraded result.

rule.statement: Skip when no reranker is used or only one backend exists forever.
rule.rationale: Stub rule referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml when none of the qualifying branches fire.
